name: sqlspec
documentation: # SQLSpec SQL Experiments in Python ## Minimal SQL Abstractions for Python. - Modern: Typed and Extensible - Multi-database: SQLite, Postgres, DuckDB, MySQL, Oracle, SQL Server, Spanner, Big Query, and more... - Easy ability to manipulate and add filters to queries - Validate and Convert between dialects with `sqlglot` - and more... ## Can it do `X`? - Probably not currently; but, if it makes sense we can add enhancements. ## Inspiration `aiosql` is the primary influence for this library. However, I wanted to be able to use the query interface from `aiosql` a bit more flexibly. Why not add it to `aiosql`? Where it makes sense, many of these changes will likely get submitted to aiosql as a PR (`spanner` and `bigquery` drivers are likely the starting point.)

modules.name: sqlspec._typing

This is a simple wrapper around a few important classes in each library.

This is used to ensure compatibility when one or more of the libraries are installed.

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.duckdb.config.DuckDBConfig.database
documentation: The path to the database file to be opened. Pass ":memory:" to open a connection to a database that resides in RAM instead of on disk. If not specified, an in-memory database will be created.

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.duckdb.config.DuckDBConfig.read_only
documentation: If True, the database will be opened in read-only mode. This is required if multiple processes want to access the same database file at the same time.

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.duckdb.config.DuckDBConfig.config
documentation:
A dictionary of configuration options to be passed to DuckDB. These can include settings like 'access_mode', 'max_memory', 'threads', etc.

For details see: https://duckdb.org/docs/api/python/overview#connection-options

Configuration for DuckDB database connections.

This class provides configuration options for DuckDB database connections, wrapping all parameters
available to duckdb.connect().

For details see: https://duckdb.org/docs/api/python/overview#connection-options

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.aiosqlite.config.AiosqliteConfig.database
documentation: The path to the database file to be opened. Pass ":memory:" to open a connection to a database that resides in RAM instead of on disk.

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.aiosqlite.config.AiosqliteConfig.timeout
documentation: How many seconds the connection should wait before raising an OperationalError when a table is locked. If another thread or process has acquired a shared lock, a wait for the specified timeout occurs.

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.aiosqlite.config.AiosqliteConfig.detect_types
documentation: Control whether and how data types are detected. It can be 0 (default) or a combination of PARSE_DECLTYPES and PARSE_COLNAMES.

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.aiosqlite.config.AiosqliteConfig.isolation_level
documentation: The isolation_level of the connection. This can be None for autocommit mode or one of "DEFERRED", "IMMEDIATE" or "EXCLUSIVE".

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.aiosqlite.config.AiosqliteConfig.check_same_thread
documentation: If True (default), ProgrammingError is raised if the database connection is used by a thread other than the one that created it. If False, the connection may be shared across multiple threads.

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.aiosqlite.config.AiosqliteConfig.factory
documentation: A custom Connection class factory. If given, must be a callable that returns a Connection instance.

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.aiosqlite.config.AiosqliteConfig.cached_statements
documentation: The number of statements that SQLite will cache for this connection. The default is 128.

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.aiosqlite.config.AiosqliteConfig.uri
documentation: If set to True, database is interpreted as a URI with supported options.

Configuration for Aiosqlite database connections.

This class provides configuration options for Aiosqlite database connections, wrapping all parameters
available to aiosqlite.connect().

For details see: https://github.com/omnilib/aiosqlite/blob/main/aiosqlite/__init__.pyi

Configuration for Oracle database connection pools.

This class provides configuration options for both synchronous and asynchronous Oracle
database connection pools. It supports all standard Oracle connection parameters and pool-specific
settings.([1](https://python-oracledb.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api_manual/module.html))

classes.name: sqlspec.adapters.oracledb.config._common.OracleGenericDatabaseConfig
documentation:
Oracle database Configuration.

This class provides the base configuration for Oracle database connections, extending
the generic database configuration with Oracle-specific settings. It supports both
thin and thick modes of the python-oracledb driver.([1](https://python-oracledb.readthedocs.io/en/latest/index.html))

The configuration supports all standard Oracle connection parameters and can be used
with both synchronous and asynchronous connections. It includes support for features
like Oracle Wallet, external authentication, connection pooling, and advanced security
options.([2](https://python-oracledb.readthedocs.io/en/latest/user_guide/tuning.html))

Configuration for Asyncmy's connection pool.

This class provides configuration options for Asyncmy database connection pools.

For details see: https://github.com/long2ice/asyncmy

classes.name: sqlspec.adapters.asyncmy.config.AsyncMyConfig

methods.name: sqlspec.adapters.asyncmy.config.AsyncMyConfig.pool_config_dict
documentation:
Return the pool configuration as a dict.

Returns:
    A string keyed dict of config kwargs for the Asyncmy create_pool function.

methods.name: sqlspec.adapters.asyncmy.config.AsyncMyConfig.create_pool
documentation:
Return a pool. If none exists yet, create one.

Returns:
    Getter that returns the pool instance used by the plugin.

Raises:
    ImproperConfigurationError: If the pool could not be created.

methods.name: sqlspec.adapters.asyncmy.config.AsyncMyConfig.provide_pool
documentation:
Create a pool instance.

Returns:
    A Pool instance.

methods.name: sqlspec.adapters.asyncmy.config.AsyncMyConfig.lifespan
documentation:
Manage the lifecycle of a database connection pool.

Yields:
    None

Raises:
    ImproperConfigurationError: If the pool could not be established.

methods.name: sqlspec.adapters.asyncmy.config.AsyncMyConfig.provide_connection
documentation:
Create and provide a database connection.

Yields:
    An Asyncmy connection instance.

Raises:
    ImproperConfigurationError: If the connection could not be established.

If set, the plugin will use the provided pool rather than instantiate one.

classes.name: sqlspec.adapters.sqlite.config.SqliteConfig

methods.name: sqlspec.adapters.sqlite.config.SqliteConfig.connection_config_dict
documentation:
Return the connection configuration as a dict.

Returns:
    A string keyed dict of config kwargs for the sqlite3.connect() function.

methods.name: sqlspec.adapters.sqlite.config.SqliteConfig.create_connection
documentation:
Create and return a new database connection.

Returns:
    A new SQLite connection instance.

Raises:
    ImproperConfigurationError: If the connection could not be established.

methods.name: sqlspec.adapters.sqlite.config.SqliteConfig.lifespan
documentation:
Manage the lifecycle of a database connection.

Yields:
    None

Raises:
    ImproperConfigurationError: If the connection could not be established.

methods.name: sqlspec.adapters.sqlite.config.SqliteConfig.provide_connection
documentation:
Create and provide a database connection.

Yields:
    A SQLite connection instance.

Raises:
    ImproperConfigurationError: If the connection could not be established.

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.sqlite.config.SqliteConfig.database
documentation: The path to the database file to be opened. Pass ":memory:" to open a connection to a database that resides in RAM instead of on disk.

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.sqlite.config.SqliteConfig.timeout
documentation: How many seconds the connection should wait before raising an OperationalError when a table is locked. If another thread or process has acquired a shared lock, a wait for the specified timeout occurs.

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.sqlite.config.SqliteConfig.detect_types
documentation: Control whether and how data types are detected. It can be 0 (default) or a combination of PARSE_DECLTYPES and PARSE_COLNAMES.

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.sqlite.config.SqliteConfig.isolation_level
documentation: The isolation_level of the connection. This can be None for autocommit mode or one of "DEFERRED", "IMMEDIATE" or "EXCLUSIVE".

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.sqlite.config.SqliteConfig.check_same_thread
documentation: If True (default), ProgrammingError is raised if the database connection is used by a thread other than the one that created it. If False, the connection may be shared across multiple threads.

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.sqlite.config.SqliteConfig.factory
documentation: A custom Connection class factory. If given, must be a callable that returns a Connection instance.

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.sqlite.config.SqliteConfig.cached_statements
documentation: The number of statements that SQLite will cache for this connection. The default is 128.

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.sqlite.config.SqliteConfig.uri
documentation: If set to True, database is interpreted as a URI with supported options.

Configuration for SQLite database connections.

This class provides configuration options for SQLite database connections, wrapping all parameters
available to sqlite3.connect().

For details see: https://docs.python.org/3/library/sqlite3.html#sqlite3.connect

classes.name: sqlspec.adapters.psycopg.config._common.PsycoPgGenericPoolConfig

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.psycopg.config._common.PsycoPgGenericPoolConfig.conninfo
documentation: Connection string in libpq format

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.psycopg.config._common.PsycoPgGenericPoolConfig.kwargs
documentation: Additional connection parameters

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.psycopg.config._common.PsycoPgGenericPoolConfig.min_size
documentation: Minimum number of connections in the pool

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.psycopg.config._common.PsycoPgGenericPoolConfig.max_size
documentation: Maximum number of connections in the pool

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.psycopg.config._common.PsycoPgGenericPoolConfig.name
documentation: Name of the connection pool

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.psycopg.config._common.PsycoPgGenericPoolConfig.timeout
documentation: Timeout for acquiring connections

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.psycopg.config._common.PsycoPgGenericPoolConfig.max_waiting
documentation: Maximum number of waiting clients

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.psycopg.config._common.PsycoPgGenericPoolConfig.max_lifetime
documentation: Maximum connection lifetime

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.psycopg.config._common.PsycoPgGenericPoolConfig.max_idle
documentation: Maximum idle time for connections

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.psycopg.config._common.PsycoPgGenericPoolConfig.reconnect_timeout
documentation: Time between reconnection attempts

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.psycopg.config._common.PsycoPgGenericPoolConfig.num_workers
documentation: Number of background workers

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.psycopg.config._common.PsycoPgGenericPoolConfig.configure
documentation: Callback to configure new connections

Configuration for Psycopg connection pools.

This class provides configuration options for both synchronous and asynchronous Psycopg
database connection pools. It supports all standard Psycopg connection parameters and pool-specific
settings.([1](https://www.psycopg.org/psycopg3/docs/api/pool.html))

classes.name: sqlspec.adapters.psycopg.config._common.PsycoPgGenericDatabaseConfig
documentation:
Psycopg database Configuration.

This class provides the base configuration for Psycopg database connections, extending
the generic database configuration with Psycopg-specific settings.([1](https://www.psycopg.org/psycopg3/docs/api/connections.html))

The configuration supports all standard Psycopg connection parameters and can be used
with both synchronous and asynchronous connections.([2](https://www.psycopg.org/psycopg3/docs/api/connections.html))

classes.name: sqlspec.adapters.asyncpg.config.AsyncPgPoolConfig

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.asyncpg.config.AsyncPgPoolConfig.dsn
documentation: Connection arguments specified using as a single string in the following format: `postgres://user:pass@host:port/database?option=value`

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.asyncpg.config.AsyncPgPoolConfig.connect_kwargs
documentation: A dictionary of arguments which will be passed directly to the `connect()` method as keyword arguments.

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.asyncpg.config.AsyncPgPoolConfig.connection_class
documentation: The class to use for connections. Must be a subclass of Connection

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.asyncpg.config.AsyncPgPoolConfig.record_class
documentation: If specified, the class to use for records returned by queries on the connections in this pool. Must be a subclass of Record.

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.asyncpg.config.AsyncPgPoolConfig.min_size
documentation: The number of connections to keep open inside the connection pool.

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.asyncpg.config.AsyncPgPoolConfig.max_size
documentation:
The number of connections to allow in connection pool “overflow”, that is connections that can be opened above
and beyond the pool_size setting, which defaults to 10.

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.asyncpg.config.AsyncPgPoolConfig.max_queries
documentation: Number of queries after a connection is closed and replaced with a new connection.

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.asyncpg.config.AsyncPgPoolConfig.max_inactive_connection_lifetime
documentation: Number of seconds after which inactive connections in the pool will be closed. Pass 0 to disable this mechanism.

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.asyncpg.config.AsyncPgPoolConfig.setup
documentation: A coroutine to prepare a connection right before it is returned from Pool.acquire(). An example use case would be to automatically set up notifications listeners for all connections of a pool.

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.asyncpg.config.AsyncPgPoolConfig.init
documentation: A coroutine to prepare a connection right before it is returned from Pool.acquire(). An example use case would be to automatically set up notifications listeners for all connections of a pool.

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.asyncpg.config.AsyncPgPoolConfig.loop
documentation: An asyncio event loop instance. If None, the default event loop will be used.

Configuration for Asyncpg's {class}`Pool <asyncpg.pool.Pool>`.

For details see: <https://magicstack.github.io/asyncpg/current/api/index.html#connection-pools>

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.asyncpg.config.AsyncPgConfig.json_deserializer
documentation:
For dialects that support the {class}`JSON <sqlalchemy.types.JSON>` datatype, this is a Python callable that will
convert a JSON string to a Python object. By default, this is set to SQLSpec's
{attr}`decode_json() <sqlspec._serialization.decode_json>` function.

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.asyncpg.config.AsyncPgConfig.json_serializer
documentation:
For dialects that support the JSON datatype, this is a Python callable that will render a given object as JSON.
By default, SQLSpec's {attr}`encode_json() <sqlspec._serialization.encode_json>` is used.

class_variables.name: sqlspec.adapters.asyncpg.config.AsyncPgConfig.pool_instance
documentation:
Optional pool to use.

If set, the plugin will use the provided pool rather than instantiate one.
Asyncpg Configuration.

functions.name: sqlspec.utils.dataclass.is_dataclass_instance
documentation:
Check if an object is a dataclass instance.

Args:
    obj: An object to check.

Returns:
    True if the object is a dataclass instance.

functions.name: sqlspec.utils.dataclass.extract_dataclass_fields
documentation:
Extract dataclass fields.

Args:
    dt: A dataclass instance.
    exclude_none: Whether to exclude None values.
    exclude_empty: Whether to exclude Empty values.
    include: An iterable of fields to include.
    exclude: An iterable of fields to exclude.


Returns:
    A tuple of dataclass fields.

functions.name: sqlspec.utils.dataclass.extract_dataclass_items
documentation:
Extract dataclass name, value pairs.

Unlike the 'asdict' method exports by the stdlib, this function does not pickle values.

Args:
    dt: A dataclass instance.
    exclude_none: Whether to exclude None values.
    exclude_empty: Whether to exclude Empty values.
    include: An iterable of fields to include.
    exclude: An iterable of fields to exclude.

Returns:
    A tuple of key/value pairs.

functions.name: sqlspec.utils.dataclass.simple_asdict
documentation:
Convert a dataclass to a dictionary.

This method has important differences to the standard library version:
- it does not deepcopy values
- it does not recurse into collections

Args:
    obj: A dataclass instance.
    exclude_none: Whether to exclude None values.
    exclude_empty: Whether to exclude Empty values.
    convert_nested: Whether to recursively convert nested dataclasses.
    exclude: An iterable of fields to exclude.

Returns:
    A dictionary of key/value pairs.

documentation: Collection filter datastructures.

classes.name: sqlspec.filters.BeforeAfter

class_variables.name: sqlspec.filters.BeforeAfter.field_name
documentation: Name of the model attribute to filter on.

class_variables.name: sqlspec.filters.BeforeAfter.before
documentation: Filter results where field earlier than this.

class_variables.name: sqlspec.filters.BeforeAfter.after
documentation: Filter results where field later than this.
Data required to filter a query on a `datetime` column.

classes.name: sqlspec.filters.OnBeforeAfter

documentation: Name of the model attribute to filter on.

class_variables.name: sqlspec.filters.OnBeforeAfter.on_or_before
documentation: Filter results where field is on or earlier than this.

class_variables.name: sqlspec.filters.OnBeforeAfter.on_or_after
documentation: Filter results where field on or later than this.
Data required to filter a query on a `datetime` column.

classes.name: sqlspec.filters.InAnyFilter
documentation: Subclass for methods that have a `prefer_any` attribute.

classes.name: sqlspec.filters.CollectionFilter

class_variables.name: sqlspec.filters.CollectionFilter.field_name
documentation: Name of the model attribute to filter on.

class_variables.name: sqlspec.filters.CollectionFilter.values
documentation:
Values for `IN` clause.

An empty list will return an empty result set, however, if `None`, the filter is not applied to the query, and all rows are returned.
Data required to construct a `WHERE ... IN (...)` clause.

classes.name: sqlspec.filters.NotInCollectionFilter

class_variables.name: sqlspec.filters.NotInCollectionFilter.field_name
documentation: Name of the model attribute to filter on.

class_variables.name: sqlspec.filters.NotInCollectionFilter.values
documentation:
Values for `NOT IN` clause.

An empty list or `None` will return all rows.
Data required to construct a `WHERE ... NOT IN (...)` clause.

classes.name: sqlspec.filters.PaginationFilter
documentation: Subclass for methods that function as a pagination type.

classes.name: sqlspec.filters.LimitOffset

class_variables.name: sqlspec.filters.LimitOffset.limit
documentation: Value for `LIMIT` clause of query.

class_variables.name: sqlspec.filters.LimitOffset.offset
documentation: Value for `OFFSET` clause of query.
Data required to add limit/offset filtering to a query.

classes.name: sqlspec.filters.OrderBy

class_variables.name: sqlspec.filters.OrderBy.field_name
documentation: Name of the model attribute to sort on.

class_variables.name: sqlspec.filters.OrderBy.sort_order
documentation: Sort ascending or descending
Data required to construct a `ORDER BY ...` clause.

classes.name: sqlspec.filters.SearchFilter

class_variables.name: sqlspec.filters.SearchFilter.field_name
documentation: Name of the model attribute to search on.

class_variables.name: sqlspec.filters.SearchFilter.value
documentation: Search value.

class_variables.name: sqlspec.filters.SearchFilter.ignore_case
documentation: Should the search be case insensitive.
Data required to construct a `WHERE field_name LIKE '%' || :value || '%'` clause.

classes.name: sqlspec.filters.NotInSearchFilter
documentation: Data required to construct a `WHERE field_name NOT LIKE '%' || :value || '%'` clause.
